new_classes.definition_en: A spirit beverage produced by distillation or rectification of ethyl alcohol of agricultural origin, having characteristic organoleptic qualities with no predominating flavour other than that of the raw material; classified under CN subheading 2208 60.

new_classes.label_de: Wodka in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (Unterposition 2208 60 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Vodka as described under CN heading 2208 60, presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres; classified under CN subheading 2208 60 11.

The production process for vodka, consisting of distillation or rectification of ethyl alcohol of agricultural origin, resulting in a spirit with no predominating flavour other than that of the raw material.

new_classes.label_en: Vodka characteristic quality (no predominating flavour other than raw material)
new_classes.label_de: Wodka-charakteristische Qualität (kein dominierender Aromastoff außer dem Ausgangsprodukt)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by vodka indicating that it possesses characteristic organoleptic properties with no predominating flavour other than that of the raw material, as required by the HS explanatory notes for heading 2208, paragraph 3, item 5.

coverage_explanation:
The key distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) vodka as a subclass of SpiritsBeverages2208; (2) the container volume threshold (≤ 2 L) for subheading 22086011 via a haspart SmallContainer restriction; (3) the characteristic production process (distillation/rectification of agricultural ethyl alcohol); (4) the organoleptic quality (no predominating flavour other than raw material). 

Coverage gaps reducing the score: (a) The legal note references HS Explanatory Notes heading 2208 paragraph 3 item 5, which specifies detailed raw-material criteria (agricultural ethyl alcohol, including potato, grain or other agricultural origin) that could further discriminate vodka sub-types – this raw-material specificity is not modelled. (b) The sibling subheading 22086091 (containers > 2 L) is not modelled here (it falls under a different CN code). (c) The disjointness axioms on process classes are expressed using an incorrect pattern (complement via RO_0000086, which is not semantically correct for disjointness between process classes) – the OWL complement restriction pattern here is used as a proxy given the tooling constraint, but a proper owl:disjointWith between process classes would be cleaner. These gaps reduce coverage to approximately 0.75.
